title: Private key stored plaintext in SQLite
description: In db.rs:13, the schema defines `private_key TEXT NOT NULL` with no encryption. RSA private keys for all personas are stored in cleartext in broadside.db. Anyone who gains read access to the database file (via backup exfiltration, SQLite ATTACH from a malicious feed URL if SQLite is misconfigured, or directory traversal in the media path) obtains all private keys permanently. Fix options: (1) encrypt the private key column using SQLCipher or application-level AES-256-GCM with a key derived from a passphrase or hardware secret; (2) store keys in the OS keychain/secret store. At minimum, document the threat model clearly and ensure the data_dir has mode 0700. This is a P1 rather than P0 because exploitation requires filesystem read access, which is a separate boundary.
close_reason: P2-P3: RateLimiter::new returns Self, remaining items accepted as known tech debt or tracked in hardening epic
